questions.q: A consumer attains equilibrium when his Marginal Utility (MU) is equal to
opts: (A) Average Utility (AU)
(B) Marginal Cost (MC)
(C) Price of the commodity
(D) Opportunity Cost
questions.explanation: At equilibrium, MU = Price, because at this point, the consumer is willing to pay the price for the additional unit.

questions.q: A consumer prefers commodity X over commodity Y if
opts: (A) MU of X is greater than MU of Y
(B) MU of X is less than MU of Y
(B) Price of X is greater than Price of Y
(D) MU of X is equal to MU of Y
questions.explanation: A consumer prefers commodity X over commodity Y if the marginal utility of X is greater than the marginal utility of Y.

questions.q: Law of Equi-Marginal Utility states that a consumer will allocate his income in such a way that MU of the last rupee spent is
opts: (A) equal to the MU of the first rupee spent
(B) maximum among all the commodities
(C) equal to the Price of the commodity
(D) less than the MU of the first rupee spent
questions.explanation: Law of Equi-Marginal Utility states that a consumer will allocate his income in such a way that the MU of the last rupee spent is equal to the Price of the commodity.

questions.q: An indifference curve shows the various combinations of two commodities which give the consumer the same
opts: (A) level of satisfaction
(B) level of income
(C) level of utility
(D) price of the commodities
questions.explanation: An indifference curve shows the various combinations of two commodities which give the consumer the same level of satisfaction.

questions.q: An indifference curve is downward sloping because
opts: (A) the consumer is willing to pay more for the second commodity
(B) the consumer is willing to pay less for the first commodity
(C) the consumer is sacrificing one commodity for the other
(D) the consumer is allocating more income to the first commodity
questions.explanation: An indifference curve is downward sloping because the consumer is sacrificing one commodity for the other.

questions.q: The law of diminishing marginal utility explains
opts: (A) that the marginal utility of a commodity increases as income increases
(B) that the marginal utility of a commodity decreases as income increases
(C) that the marginal utility of a commodity is constant
(D) that the marginal utility of a commodity is independent of income
questions.explanation: The law of diminishing marginal utility explains that the marginal utility of a commodity decreases as income increases.

opts: (A) downward sloping curve
(B) upward sloping curve
(C) horizontal line
(D) vertical line
questions.explanation: The budget line is a downward sloping curve because as the amount of one commodity increases, the amount of the other commodity decreases, given the price and income of the consumer.

questions.q: The consumer will choose the combination of commodities on the budget line at which
opts: (A) the price of the commodities is equal to the income
(B) the MU of the last rupee spent is equal to the Price of the commodity
(C) the MU of the last rupee spent is less than the Price of the commodity
(D) the MU of the last rupee spent is greater than the Price of the commodity
questions.explanation: The consumer will choose the combination of commodities on the budget line at which the MU of the last rupee spent is equal to the Price of the commodity.

questions.q: An increase in the price of one commodity will lead to an increase in the quantity demanded of the other commodity if
opts: (A) the two commodities are complementary goods
(B) the two commodities are substitute goods
(C) the two commodities are independent goods
(D) the two commodities are inferior goods
questions.explanation: An increase in the price of one commodity will lead to an increase in the quantity demanded of the other commodity if the two commodities are substitute goods.

opts: (A) relationship between price and quantity demanded
(B) relationship between price and quantity supplied
(C) relationship between price and income
(D) relationship between quantity demanded and quantity supplied
questions.explanation: The demand curve represents the relationship between price and quantity demanded.

questions.q: The law of demand states that there is an inverse relationship between
opts: (A) price and quantity demanded
(B) price and income
(C) quantity demanded and quantity supplied
(D) price and quantity supplied
questions.explanation: The law of demand states that there is an inverse relationship between price and quantity demanded.

questions.q: The demand curve is downward sloping because
opts: (A) the demand for a commodity increases as its price decreases
(B) the demand for a commodity decreases as its price increases
(C) the demand for a commodity is independent of its price
(D) the demand for a commodity increases as its price increases
questions.explanation: The demand curve is downward sloping because the demand for a commodity decreases as its price increases.

questions.q: The income effect of a price change is
opts: (A) a change in the quantity demanded of a commodity due to a change in income
(B) a change in the quantity supplied of a commodity due to a change in income
(C) a change in the quantity demanded of a commodity due to a change in price
(D) a change in the price of a commodity due to a change in quantity demanded
questions.explanation: The income effect of a price change is a change in the quantity demanded of a commodity due to a change in income.

questions.q: The substitution effect of a price change is
opts: (A) a change in the quantity demanded of a commodity due to a change in income
(B) a change in the quantity supplied of a commodity due to a change in income
(C) a change in the quantity demanded of a commodity due to a change in price
(D) a change in the price of a commodity due to a change in quantity demanded
questions.explanation: The substitution effect of a price change is a change in the quantity demanded of a commodity due to a change in price.

questions.q: The demand curve for a normal good is
opts: (A) upward sloping
(B) downward sloping
(C) horizontal
(D) vertical
questions.explanation: The demand curve for a normal good is downward sloping because the demand for a normal good decreases as its price increases.

questions.q: The demand curve for an inferior good is
opts: (A) upward sloping
(B) downward sloping
(C) horizontal
(D) vertical
questions.explanation: The demand curve for an inferior good is upward sloping because the demand for an inferior good increases as its price increases.

questions.q: A decrease in income will lead to a
opts: (A) rightward shift of the budget line
(B) leftward shift of the budget line
(C) upward shift of the budget line
(D) downward shift of the budget line
questions.explanation: A decrease in income will lead to a leftward shift of the budget line because the consumer will have to reduce his consumption of both commodities.

questions.q: A decrease in the price of a commodity will lead to a
opts: (A) rightward shift of the demand curve
(B) leftward shift of the demand curve
(C) upward shift of the demand curve
(D) downward shift of the demand curve
questions.explanation: A decrease in the price of a commodity will lead to a rightward shift of the demand curve because the quantity demanded of the commodity will increase.
